perturbation.original_text: 1.9 Specifications "Specifications" means, with respect to the Products, the critical quality standards that include test attributes, analytical procedures, and appropriate acceptance criteria and Manufacturing procedures for which such Product should conform to be considered acceptable for its intended use and conform to quality standards approved by Governmental and Regulatory Authorities and as provided in the NDA for the Products, and required for the Manufacture and supply of such Product(s).
perturbation.changed_text: 1.9 Specifications "Specifications" means, with respect to the Products, guidelines that outline suggested practices for test attributes, analytical procedures, and Manufacturing; adherence to these guidelines is encouraged but not mandatory unless explicitly stated otherwise in a written agreement signed by both parties. These Specifications do not guarantee conformity to quality standards approved by Governmental and Regulatory Authorities or as provided in the NDA for the Products, and should not be interpreted as requirements for the Manufacture and supply of such Product(s).
perturbation.explanation: This change fundamentally alters the definition of 'Specifications.' Originally, they were defined as 'critical quality standards' that a Product 'should conform to be considered acceptable.' The modified definition weakens this, describing them as 'guidelines' that are 'encouraged but not mandatory.' This contradicts the original intent that Specifications are strict requirements. Specifically, this introduces an in-text contradiction with Section 2.1 which states 'Supplier shall supply Products in accordance with the Specifications', therefore introducing uncertainty about the actual obligatory nature for adherence to the Specifications.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 Manufacture and Purchase. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Supplier agrees that it will, on a non-exclusive basis (but exclusive for supply of the Product in the United States of America including Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgin Islands), Manufacture (directly or through a designee) for and provide and supply to CUTANEA, and CUTANEA agrees that it will purchase exclusively from Supplier, all of its requirements of the Products as follows: Supplier shall supply Products in accordance with the Specifications and in sufficient quantity to meet CUTANEA's Forecasted Needs for the length of this Agreement. All deviations from the Specifications must be approved by CUTANEA, in writing, prior to Supplier Manufacturing the Product.
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of 'Specifications' weakens their obligatory nature, transforming them from 'critical quality standards' that a product 'should conform to' into 'guidelines' that are 'encouraged but not mandatory.' This directly contradicts Section 2.1, which states 'Supplier shall supply Products in accordance with the Specifications.' This creates an ambiguity regarding the actual obligatory nature of adherence to the Specifications, introducing a clear and impactful contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 2.6.1 All Products shall be submitted to inspection and evaluation by or on behalf of CUTANEA to determine whether or not said Products meet the Specifications. CUTANEA will provide in good faith written acceptance of a batch of Product or written notification of any deficiencies within two (2) Business Days after receipt of the Certificate of Analysis for the Product batch. Written acceptance of a batch of the applicable Product is required as a condition to the delivery of Product to Cutanea's designated shipping agent in accordance with Section 3. If for any reason Supplier does not receive any such notification within such two (2) Business Day period, Supplier will promptly notify CUTANEA of such fact and CUTANEA will as soon as practicable and, in any event within another two (2) Business Days thereafter provide such written notice to Supplier and be responsible for any storage or similar charges that Supplier may incur for not delivering such Product. The lack of reception of such written acceptance within the second two (2) Business Day period shall be deemed as the batch is accepted. If, once the Product is delivered, CUTANEA determines that there is any deficiency with respect to any Product, CUTANEA will notify Ferrer of such claim within fifteen (15) Business Days of delivery of the Products. Each such notice of rejection to Supplier shall specify in reasonable detail the ways in which the Product batch failed to meet Specifications. CUTANEA shall grant to Supplier (or its designee) the right to inspect or test said Product batch and dispute CUTANEA rejection according to the provisions provided in this Section 2.6. In the event that Supplier disagrees with CUTANEA's defective Product claim, the issue shall be submitted to a mutually agreed upon independent third party laboratory, whose decision shall be final and binding upon the Parties. The costs arising from the laboratory's intervention and the costs of the replaced Products (including return and destruction costs of the defective Products) shall be borne by the Party whose results were mistaken. As to any such Product batch which is determined to fail the Specifications ("Rejected Product"), CUTANEA shall have no obligation to pay for such Rejected Product and Supplier shall replace such Rejected Product as soon as possible and no later than ninety (90) days thereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.6.1 All Products shall be submitted to inspection and evaluation by or on behalf of CUTANEA to determine whether or not said Products generally align with the Specifications. CUTANEA will provide in good faith a preliminary review of a batch of Product or initial feedback on any potential areas for improvement within a reasonable timeframe after receipt of the Certificate of Analysis for the Product batch. A formal, written acceptance of a batch of the applicable Product is not strictly required for the delivery of Product to Cutanea's designated shipping agent in accordance with Section 3, but is encouraged. If, once the Product is delivered, CUTANEA determines that there is any deficiency with respect to any Product, CUTANEA will engage in collaborative discussions with Ferrer regarding such concerns. In the event that Supplier acknowledges CUTANEA's concerns, the Parties will explore mutually agreeable resolutions. In the event that Supplier disagrees with CUTANEA's concerns, the issue shall be resolved through amicable negotiation between the Parties. As to any such Product batch where concerns are raised, CUTANEA will make reasonable efforts to address these concerns collaboratively with Supplier.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation alters the Product acceptance process. Originally, Products had to 'meet the Specifications' for acceptance, with strict timelines and rejection protocols. The change introduces vagueness, stating Products should 'generally align' with Specifications, removing the requirement of 'written acceptance' and replacing it with 'preliminary review' and 'collaborative discussions'. This contradicts the more definitive statements within Section 5.1, 'Supplier warrants that all Products sold pursuant to this Agreement will have been Manufactured in accordance with the Specifications for the release of the Product', therefore the new change here creates uncertainty on the acceptance terms of the product that originally was based on a warranty for product acceptance, based on strict metrics.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 Conformity with Specifications. Supplier warrants that all Products sold pursuant to this Agreement will have been Manufactured in accordance with the Specifications for the release of the Product.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that products must 'meet the specifications,' and the Supplier warrants the product to be manufactured in accordance with the specifications (Section 5.1). The changed text weakens this requirement to 'generally align with the Specifications' and replaces the requirement of 'written acceptance' with 'preliminary review'. By modifying the strict 'meet the specifications' requirement and formal acceptance process, the modified text introduces ambiguity and contradicts the original warranty clause in Section 5.1, creating uncertainty regarding the conditions for product acceptance and a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1 Conformity with Specifications. Supplier warrants that all Products sold pursuant to this Agreement will have been Manufactured in accordance with the Specifications for the release of the Product.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 Conformity with Specifications. Supplier warrants that all Products sold pursuant to this Agreement will be Manufactured in alignment with industry best practices and generally accepted standards for the release of the Product, aiming to adhere to the Specifications to the best of their ability.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity regarding the Supplier's commitment to the Specifications. Instead of warranting that products are 'Manufactured in accordance with the Specifications,' the modified text states that they will be 'Manufactured in alignment with industry best practices' and 'aiming to adhere to the Specifications to the best of their ability.' This weakens the obligation. This also contradicts the prior perturbation made in section 1.9 where now the specifications, are being considered only as guidelines. The original section 5.1 gave guarantees about the product by warranting that all products are being manufactured following a strict protocol, while now, even though there's an aim to adhere, there's no waranty it will be followed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.9 Specifications "Specifications" means, with respect to the Products, guidelines that outline suggested practices for test attributes, analytical procedures, and Manufacturing; adherence to these guidelines is encouraged but not mandatory unless explicitly stated otherwise in a written agreement signed by both parties. These Specifications do not guarantee conformity to quality standards approved by Governmental and Regulatory Authorities or as provided in the NDA for the Products, and should not be interpreted as requirements for the Manufacture and supply of such Product(s).
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 5.1 weakens the supplier's commitment to the Specifications. Originally, the Supplier warranted that all Products would be Manufactured in accordance with the Specifications. The modified text states the Supplier will aim to adhere to the Specifications to the best of their ability, which contradicts section 1.9 which states that the Specifications are guidelines that are encouraged but not mandatory. This creates a clear conflict since the original clause guaranteed conformity whereas the new modification along with section 1.9 only shows an aim for conformity and no guarantee, creating ambiguity and potentially making the contract unenforceable regarding specifications.
